content_of_post_to_comment: Hello Everyone Just Wanted to Share my Thoughts.Internet is great for many things what i love about it for the most part is that i discovered a lot of great movies tv shows Video Games ,art and useful informations there is also lot of great content in Youtube but on the other hand internet addiction made my mental health worse i have ADHD and Social Anxiety and you know internet at this point feels like its own separate world and i feel mentally stuck in that world today it feels almost impossible life without internet you need internet like you need water or food you want to watch a movie you need a internet you want to play a video game you need internet you want to listen music you need a internet i cant imagine my day without using internet ...you know there is this quote ignorance is a bliss less you know better you sleep there was a time when i was naive and optimistic everything felt exciting and there was this sense of wonder and excitement now its gone i dont know why i am obsessed what other people think for example with Video Games back then i enjoyed a lot of video games i didnt care if the game wasnt a masterpiece or the game didnt have the best graphics i had fun but during last decade i watched a lot of game reviews game discussions now i hardly enjoy video games i became game critic oh this game is repetitive this doesn´t run 6ofps this game is simple blah blah social media is near irredeemable garbage is i remember not wanting to be in Facebook i regret spending so much time on Facebook i also consumed way too many toxic content for example Anti Woke Content ohhh this movie is woke this game is woke this is a feminist propaganda and now i´m sick of it i dont want to watch any of that..internet realy exposes just how awful humans can be bullies narcisists arrogant spreading misinformation...you know i tried to minimalize my time internet but every time i get bored i got straight to internet i cant stop thinking overthinking i´m constantly arguing about things i´ve read online in my head
comment: Spending so much time on reviews and online discourse before actually playing is a sneaky way games lose their magic. I went through something similar with music and films, everything started feeling like content to evaluate instead of experience. Cutting off that input for a while helped me actually feel boredom again without instantly reaching for a feed.
